course_description:
Through a professional experience block placement this course provides a further introduction to the practice of teaching and demonstrates the ongoing importance of creativity, initiative and personal autonomy in teaching. The professional experience provides opportunities for purposeful and insightful supervised experiences to develop preservice teachers' understandings of themselves as teachers, the students they will teach, the contexts in which they will teach and the teaching/learning process. Observation of practice will continue to provide preservice teachers with opportunities to critically reflect on and improve their own practice. Another feature of the course will be the ongoing practice in the design and implementation of units/lessons and assessment. This course is developmental and builds on EDUC7605, providing a basis for the subsequent course EDUC7625. The course will also incorporate and implement knowledge and skills developed across the Master of Teaching (Secondary) program so far.

For Semester 1, 2025, the mid-semester break for this course will be relocated to university week 8.

NOTE (for Sem 2 offering ONLY): This course runs to the standard Semester 2 calendar for coursework, with the 20 day block professional experience commencing in revision week and concluding one week after Semester 2 central examinations.

This course measures preservice teachers’ professional growth against the Australian Professional Standards for Teachers at the graduate level.

Learning experiences are structured to challenge and support preservice teachers, to:

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate an understanding of schools as workplaces and be familiar with the statutory, ethical and other requirements that govern the work of teachers in schools. (APST: 7.1, 7.2)

Demonstrate the application of professional and disciplinary knowledge to engage with curriculum, pedagogy, assessment and reporting of student outcomes, and use this knowledge to design lesson plans and learning experiences. (APST: 2.1, 2.2, 2.3)

Create inclusive, supportive and intellectually challenging educational environments, and use a range of resources including ICT, and a range of teaching strategies that engage all learners, and set appropriate learning goals for students of all abilities and from broad range of backgrounds. (APST: 1.3, 3.1, 3.3, 3.4, 4.1)
Demonstrate a range of verbal and nonverbal communication strategies to support student engagement. (APST: 3.5)

Demonstrate skills of critical reflection to evaluate student learning and assessment data and make informed decisions regarding student learning and required modifications to teaching practices. (APST: 5.4)

Demonstrate skills of and purpose for critical reflection and engagement with APSTs, colleagues and mentors to identify professional learning needs and develop achievable plans for personal professional growth and effectively engage with constructive feedback through the stages of preservice teaching and beginning teaching. (APST: 6.1, 6.3)
Demonstrate clear, fluent, and coherent communication skills consistent with personal, professional and academic conventions.

Demonstrate all teaching and professional requirements of the professional experience placement. (APST: 1.1, 1.3, 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.5, 2.6, 3.1, 3.2, 3.3, 3.4, 3.5, 4.1, 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, 4.5, 5.1 5.2, 5.4, 6.3, 7.1, 7.2)

assessment_summary.title: AT1: Policies and procedures for professional experience
AT2: Professional experience & final evaluation report
AT3: Professional practice log & critical reflection essay

assessment_details.title: AT1: Policies and procedures for professional experience
assessment_details.task_description: This course requires you to complete a 20 day block (four week) supervised professional experience in a school. To undertake this placement, you need to: read the School of Education’s Policies and Procedures for Professional Experience document. complete and submit the acknowledgement form in Blackboard, indicating that you have read this document and will abide by these policies and procedures while representing the University of Queensland on placement. APST 7.1 & 7.2 Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Access and read the Policies and Procedures for Professional Experience document, then complete and submit the online acknowledgement in Blackboard.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
Contact the course coordinator regarding potential extension for this assessment.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessment_details.title: AT2: Professional experience & final evaluation report
assessment_details.task_description: You are required to successfully undertake a 20 day block (four weeks) of supervised professional experience in the school assigned to you by the School of Education and upload your final evaluation report to Blackboard on completion of this placement. You will be required to demonstrate appropriate teaching practice, building to full lessons and to sequences of lessons over the four week block, in specific teaching area/s. Structured observation continues. You are expected to demonstrate competence across the APSTs. To do this, you need to demonstrate the inherent requirements of the role of a teacher. At the very least, this means you will be expected to: attend the school at least half an hour before and after school start and finish times to discuss that day's and subsequent days' expectations attend any meetings held during the time of the professional experience, e.g. departmental or whole of staff meetings, or any professional development meetings where attendance is approved by the school participate in extracurricular activities, etc. available during the placement. You need to have read and to abide by the UQ School of Education's Policies and Procedures for Professional Experience Placements , including attendance and completion requirements. Refer to this document for further information. NOTE: all students are responsible for the collection and upload of their final evaluation report to Blackboard on the due date. Failure to upload your report may result in a failing grade for the course. Please do not assume that your teacher supervisor/s will forward the report to the university, or that UQ staff will upload the report to Blackboard. APST: 1.1 and 1.3; 2.1, 2.3 and 2.5-2.6; 3.1-3.5; 4.1-4.5; 5.1, 5.2 and 5.4; 6.3; 7.1, 7.2 and if appropriate to the professional experience context, 7.3. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Upload your final evaluation report via Blackboard on the due date.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment. Refer to the School of Education's Policies and Procedures for Professional Experience Placements for more information on illness preventing attendance and circumstances for which make up days and alternative completion dates can and cannot be sought.
If completing make up days within the requirements of the policies and procedures document, and this affects the final date of your placement, you will need to notify your course coordinator to ensure submission of your final evaluation report to task conditions.
Late submission
Late submission of assessment without approved extension will result in a failing grade.

assessment_details.title: AT3: Professional practice log & critical reflection essay
assessment_details.task_description: Part A – Professional practice log During your four-week professional experience, you are to keep a log of items collected, created, presented, modified or adapted, that could be used as evidence to demonstrate your fulfilment of Australian Professional Standards for Teachers (APST) Three, Four and Five from Domain Two: Professional Practice. You need to use the professional practice log provided in Blackboard to record, annotate and map your chosen items against the specific standards and focus areas. You are required to record 5 items : ideally, you should aim to include items that address multiple standards and focus areas your annotation for each item should be approximately 150-200 words explain how the item demonstrates the identified focus areas, guided by the APST Graduate level descriptors. You must attach your chosen items as appendices to your professional practice log. Part B – Critical reflection essay Write a 1000 word essay that critically reflects on the positive impact of your teaching on student learning from your four-week placement experience. Focusing on the impact of your teaching does not imply use of evidence of student growth in response to jurisdictional or national standardised tests. Rather, the focus is on demonstrating your impact on student learning as a preservice teacher. Evidence of your impact could take the form of a case study. It could include collection and interpretation of data at a student, classroom, school and/or systemic level, which you used to inform and then adjust your teaching practice to impact student learning. For example, you could analyse selected work samples from a student early and at a later stage in your placement or consult with a student of interest to inform your identification of learning needs, and then again at the end of your placement, to identify where learning or development occurred. Some items collected in Part A should be linked to your response and used as evidence to inform and prepare your critical reflection. You need to use academic literature to support your writing and APA7 referencing conventions. It is recommended that you use 6 to 8 sources throughout your response. APST 3.1-3.5, 4.1-4.5, 5.1, 5.2 and 5.4 Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Upload your submission via Blackboard by 2pm on the due date.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
Late submission of assessment without approved extension request via myUQ system will result in a failing grade.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .
assessment_details.late_submission: Late submission of assessment without approved extension request via myUQ system will result in a failing grade.

assessment_details.title: AT4: PDP & critical reflection essay
assessment_details.task_description: Part A: Personal development plan Complete a personal development plan (PDP) that identifies your professional learning needs at this stage of the Master of Teaching (Primary) program. Your PDP needs to use the PDP template provided in Blackboard to evaluate your performance and knowledge against the Australian Professional Standards for Teachers (Graduate level). Refer to feedback provided by your supervising teacher/s during placement to assist your evaluation (e.g. lesson feedback, final evaluation). Part B: Critical reflection essay Using your PDP, write a 1500 word essay that critically reflects on: your performance during the four-week supervised professional experience, and courses you have covered in this program where you need to improve your practice to better meet the learning needs of both you and your students. You should also revisit the three professional learning goals you identified in EDUC7530, and identify and explain the: ways in which your philosophy of teaching has evolved and changed since your first professional experience in EDUC7530 strategies you will put into place, before and during your final professional experience (EDUC7540), and how these strategies will enhance your practice. Using APA7 conventions, you need to reference academic literature to support your analysis of your development and the ways in which you are seeking personal growth (e.g. consider theories on reflective practice, pedagogy, learning, etc.). Using 8 to 10 sources is recommended. APST 6.1, 6.3, 6.4 Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Upload your submission via Blackboard by 2pm on the due date.

The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 2
learning_activities.topic: Preparing for the course In the course Blackboard: view introductory videos and supporting resources read the course profile, schedule and assessment tasks familiarise yourself with course readings post initial questions for your course coordinator to answer introduce yourself and complete an initial teaching reflection and questionnaire. Learning outcomes: L06

Developing your professional practice Course and assessment requirements The role of critical reflection Your supervised professional experience placement Legislative, school and employing authority requirements Ethical principles and practices in the teaching and learning process Learning outcomes: L01, L06, L08

Critically reflective and reflexive practice Case studies: meeting legislative, school and employing authority requirements Scenarios: ethical principles and practices in the teaching and learning process Critical reflection and reflexive practice: your professional experience and practice then, now and on placement. Learning outcomes: L01, L06, L07, L08

The curriculum and assessment: scope and sequence Designing teaching, learning and assessment in Queensland P to 12 Jurisdictional, sector and school-based roles and responsibilities The role of assessment and assessment data in teaching and learning Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05

Aligning teaching, learning and assessment to the curriculum Designing teaching, learning and assessment in Queensland from P to 12: accessing, understanding and using the curriculum for teaching, learning and assessment design quality assessment and the role of backward design the role of assessment data in evaluating student learning and modifying teaching practice. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05, L07, L08

The teaching, learning and assessment cycle Deliberate design — key theories and models for preparing and implementing: teaching and learning plans lesson sequences lesson stages. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L05

Iterative design, implementation, reflection and refinement Deliberate design — applying key theories and models: sample teaching, learning and assessment plans the importance of preparation, monitoring, reflection, evaluation and revision communities of practice and critical feedback. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07

Deliberate design: lesson planning and selecting resources Designing teaching and learning over days, weeks and units — theoretical and practical approaches: structuring and sequencing multiple lessons to the TLA cycle the role of monitoring, formative assessment and providing feedback on learning using supervisor feedback, critical reflection and reflexive practice to improve design and learning. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04

Designing lessons and selecting resources Explicit, directed lesson planning: alignment to the curriculum clear and achievable learning intentions and success criteria staging to activate, develop and consolidate student's knowledge, understanding and skill building, with purposeful use of teaching and learning strategies and resources use of ICTs, in ways that mitigate cybersafety issues and promote ethical and responsible use evaluative feedback, critical reflection and revision. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L06, L07, L08

Deliberate design: lesson sequences and selecting resources Designing teaching and learning over days, weeks and units — theoretical and practical approaches: structuring and sequencing multiple lessons to the TLA cycle the role of monitoring, formative assessment and providing feedback on learning using supervisor feedback, critical reflection and reflexive practice to improve design and learning. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L08

Designing lesson sequences and selecting resources Deliberate design of lesson sequences to the appropriate stage of the TLA cycle that: provide development and progression of students' knowledge, understanding and skills purposefully use a range of teaching and learning strategies and resources include and promote responsible and ethical use of ICTs provide effective data on student learning and timely, actionable feedback for learners, aligned to the curriculum use evaluative feedback, critical reflection and reflexive practice to improve design and learning. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08

Diverse learners and classroom cultures Learner diversity and strategies to create and maintain safe and supportive learning environments: legislative, school and employing authority requirements ethical principles and practices in the teaching and learning process use of the Australian Curriculum v9.0 learning progressions, of monitoring, feedback and assessment differentiating teaching and learning, Universal Design for Learning and culturally-responsive pedagogies. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05

Teaching strategies that respond to learners strengths and needs Deliberate teaching and learning design to meet the strengths and needs of diverse learners, including: use of the Australian Curriculum v9.0 learning progressions, monitoring, feedback and assessment differentiating teaching and learning, Universal Design for Learning and culturally-responsive pedagogies. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 9 To Mid Sem break
learning_activities.topic: No lectures / workshops scheduled

Managing behaviours for safe and supportive learning environments Legislative, school and employing authority requirements Ethical principles and practices in the teaching and learning process Theory and models for understanding and effectively managing learner behaviours Teacher and student communication in the classroom Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04, L05, L08

Classroom organisation and behaviour management Strategies and skills to prepare and manage safe and supportive classrooms: understanding and effectively managing learner behaviours effective classroom communication designing and organising teaching, learning and assessment communities of practice. Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08

Expectations and opportunities for professional growth Bringing it all together for the AT2 — your supervised professional experience: deliberate observation, design and implementation for teaching and learning working effectively with your school and supervising teacher/s your Final Evaluation Report. Documenting your professional growth in the AT3 and AT4: collecting evidence of student learning and teaching impact maintaining a professional log and developing a PDP writing critical reflections to task requirements. Learning outcomes: L01, L06, L08

Preparing to observe and teach Bringing it all together for the AT2: deliberate design and implementation for teaching and learning tailoring the learning, engaging learners and managing behaviours Practice scenarios Q and A: professional experience, AT3 and AT4 Learning outcomes: L01, L06, L07, L08

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 12 To Week 13
learning_activities.topic: Preparation for supervised professional experience
Supervised professional experience Four week supervised professional experience (20 days) — Monday 3 November to Friday 28 November 2025 Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library . You'll also need to be aware of the following policies and procedures while completing this course: Working with Children Policy Fitness to Practise Policy Work Integrated Learning and Work Experience Policy Student Travel Procedure Learning activities
